26/6/2026, update on 28/6/2026: TINDAKMALAYSIA NETWORK SERVICES PLT (TINDAK MALAYSIA)

Election Commission of Malaysia (SPR), Tindak Malaysia (2022), The Elections Lab, Pesaresi, Martino; Politis, Panagiotis (2023): GHS-BUILT-C R2023A - GHS Settlement Characteristics, derivedfrom Sentinel2 composite (2018) and other GHS R2023A data. European Commission, Joint Research Centre (JRC) [Dataset] doi: 10.2905/3C60DDF6-0586-4190-854B-F6AA0EDC2A30 PID: http://data.europa.eu/89h/3c60ddf6-0586-4190-854b-f6aa0edc2a30

All the percentages for electoral demographics are calculated by Tindak Malaysia using publicly available The Elections Lab data. Subsequently, Claude was used to proportionally split Others data into Bumiputera Sabah, Bumiputera Sarawak, Orang Asli and Others using Tindak Malaysia's 2022 Johor State Elections data. There is no official ethnic demographic data. Sum of percentages for electoral demographics may not attain exact 100% but the variation is kept less than 1%. The demographics data for Age Categories, Voter Type and Sex Category refers to April 2026 Electoral Roll (but not identical to the one used for Johor 2026 Electoral Roll). While total voters of April 2026 Electoral Roll and Johor 2026 Electoral Roll at state level is identical (2,727,926), we estimate the error of our data for age groups is between 0 and 0.5% for age groups of 21 and above while 2.4% for the age group of 18 - 20. Urbanisation level is derived from GHSL layer and subsequently computed by Tindak Malaysia for final urbanisation levels for each DUN. For the purpose of urbanisation classifcation, Tindak Malaysia estimated the polling district population proportionally in comparison to 2022 data. DUN N.09 Gambir is a borderline constituency between semi urban and rural status. TindakMalaysia Network Services PLT is not responsible for any errors or ommissions arising from the original data or its derivatives
